focus: traceability + correctness on ENV_REFERENCE edge-confidence flag docs and seeded-PPR stale-doc drift

findingsNew.title: Stale deleted-flag inventories still describe recovered seeded-PPR as removed
findingsNew.claim: Repo-level flag inventory docs still classify SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SEEDED_PPR_RANKING as a deleted/removed flag and say the tree no longer carries it, even though current source again defines and reads the flag after this session's recovery.
counterevidenceSought: Checked ENV_REFERENCE.md for SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SEEDED_PPR_RANKING and found no stale row there.

Checked .opencode/specs/system-code-graph/025-code-graph-core/005-seeded-ppr-ranking/spec.md:52 and found a newer correction acknowledging that the recovered flag and computeBoundedPersonalizedPageRank exist again.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The stale rows may be intended as historical records of the earlier deletion, but their current-tense inventory wording presents them as present-state flag status rather than a purely historical snapshot.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to note-only if benchmark-status.md and feature-flags.md are declared intentionally frozen historical artifacts rather than current inventory/status docs.

findingDetails.title: Stale deleted-flag inventories still describe recovered seeded-PPR as removed
findingDetails.scopeProof: Repo-wide grep for SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SEEDED_PPR_RANKING found current source definitions in code-graph-context.ts plus stale deleted/removed inventory wording in benchmark-status.md and feature-flags.md; ENV_REFERENCE.md has no stale seeded-PPR row, and the original seeded-PPR packet has a newer correction acknowledging the recovery.

traceabilityChecks.edgeConfidenceAcceptedValues: only exact lowercase true enables; unset and all other values disable

note: Documentation says default false/opt-in; code enables only when the env value is exactly true.
